description_long: This is a rectangular wall constructed from narrow, vertically oriented red bricks with thin, light gray mortar joints. The bricks are arranged in a consistent, linear pattern, giving the wall a uniform and orderly appearance. Scattered across the surface are dark stains and patches, suggesting some exposure to urban grime or weathering. The brickwork is tightly packed, with no visible gaps or openings, and the surface is mostly flat with minor textural variation from the brick faces. The coloration is predominantly a warm reddish-brown, typical of fired clay bricks, with the mortar providing a subtle contrast. The overall impression is of a sturdy, utilitarian wall commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, building exteriors, or boundary barriers.
description: A rectangular wall made of vertically aligned red bricks with light gray mortar and scattered dark stains, giving it a weathered urban look.
description_view_0: The front face shows a dense arrangement of red bricks with several dark stains concentrated in the lower and central areas.
description_view_1: This side reveals a similar brick pattern, with stains more dispersed and slightly more visible on the right edge.
description_view_2: The brickwork appears cleanest here, with fewer dark stains and a more uniform surface texture.
description_view_3: This view shows a moderate amount of staining, mostly on the upper right, with the brick pattern remaining consistent.
